"The rules we have are only those
in effect in most telephone offices. and
there must be rules to govern such
a large body of girls. The rules en-
acted seem to be the chief cause of
the trouble"


The Pacific States Telephone &
Telegraph company has its hands full
Of trouble all along the coast With
the exception of Seattle the linemen
are out along the entire system. In
San Francisco the 26 collectors en-
ployed by the company are also on
strike. In Fresno the 40 girl oper-
ators have walked out because the
manager forbid them attending la
Dor lecture.


John l. Sabin, president of the Pa
cific States Telephone & Telegraph
company, in San Francisco inter-
view states that there will be no
compromise between the company
and the striking linemen. He says
the company is losing money by the
strike, but it could AFFORD to bide it's
time and wait until the men who
walked out returned to their. work.
